element_introduction_order.mastery_context: escalation of threat scale and damage output
required checkpoint; gating progression to next area
tactical tool that must be placed and timed (detonates after delay)
secondary objective running parallel to combat progression
environmental hazards that damage on collision, adds spatial complexity

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: arcade-style escalating pressure with stage-gated releases; sustained combat rhythm punctuated by boss intensity spikes

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: POW rescue locations interspersed; treasure in Pleasure Dome bonus stage

difficulty_curve.early_game: moderate baseline with twin-stick shooter mechanics introduction
difficulty_curve.mid_game: escalation through large vehicle encounters and denser enemy formations
difficulty_curve.late_game: culmination with final boss encounter; optional Pleasure Dome requires high survivability
notable_spikes: boss encounters at end of each of 3 stages
Pleasure Dome bonus stage difficulty (treasure collection under pressure)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 3 stages progresses through scrolling combat area toward a mandatory boss encounter; boss defeat required to access next stage
Large scrolling levels distinguish Total Carnage from predecessor Smash TV's single-screen arenas; allows expansive enemy placement and vehicle deployment
POW rescue runs as secondary objective throughout stages alongside primary combat progression; increases exploration incentive within combat space
Pleasure Dome bonus stage post-game; non-mandatory treasure collection affects ending state (bugs preventing proper ending display)
